dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7983 IH: Clean Water for All Life Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Miller of Illinois (for herself, Mr. Cloud, Mr. Harrigan, Mrs. Biggs of South Carolina, Mr. Gosar, Mrs. Harshbarger, Mr. McGuire, Mr. Steube, Mr. Burchett, Ms. Boebert, Mr. Fleischmann, Mrs. Cammack, Mr. Ogles, Mr. Rose, and Mr. Norman) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend title 18, United States Code, to prohibit a chemical abortion without the physical presence of a healthcare provider.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Clean Water for All Life Act.

section.header: Chemical abortions prohibited without a health care provider physically present

subsection.text: Chapter 74 of title 18, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: in the header by striking Partial-Birth Abortions and inserting Abortion Crimes; and
by inserting at the end the following:

section.header: Chemical abortions prohibited without a health care provider physically present

subsection.text: Whoever, in or affecting interstate commerce, provides or attempts to provide abortion through a chemical abortion drug—

paragraph.text: without being physically present at the location of the chemical abortion; and
without providing a catch kit and red bag medical waste, including instructions for the patient to bring such kit and bag to the healthcare provider for proper disposal;

subsection.continuation-text: shall be imprisoned not more than 5 years, fined not more than $50,000, or both, per occurrence.

subparagraph.text: means the act of using, prescribing, administering, procuring, or selling of any instrument, medicine, drug, or any other substance, device, or means with the purpose to terminate the pregnancy of a woman, with knowledge that the termination by any of those means will with reasonable likelihood cause the death of the unborn child; and
does not mean an act described under subparagraph (A) if the act is performed with the purpose to—

clause.text: save the life or preserve the health of the unborn child;
remove a dead unborn child caused by spontaneous abortion; or

paragraph.text: The term catch kit means a collection container designed to catch and hold medical waste or infectious waste, often used for collecting samples for testing.
The term chemical abortion drug means—

subparagraph.text: mifepristone, misoprostol, mifeprex, mifegyne, or any substantially similar generic or non-generic drug or chemical dispensed for purposes of causing an abortion; or
any drug developed, marketed, sold, or distributed for the purpose of causing an abortion.

paragraph.text: The term fertilization means the fusion of a human spermatozoon with a human ovum.
The term red bag medical waste means a biohazardous waste container made to contain medical or biohazardous waste. The container must either—

subparagraph.text: be red with lettering and symbols in a contrasting color; or
include such required warning labels in fluorescent orange or orange-red, with lettering and symbols in a contrasting color.

paragraph.text: The term unborn child means an individual organism of the species homo sapiens from fertilization until live birth.

subsection.text: The table of sections for chapter 74 of title 18, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

toc.toc-entry: 1532. Chemical abortions prohibited without a health care provider physically present.
